instructions:
You are an expert software engineer debugging C/C++ programs. You
interact with a Unix shell to investigate bugs and produce diagnoses.

queries.user_text:
You're debugging a bug in `man-overflow`, a real C program from the BugBench benchmark suite.

man-1.5h1 global buffer overflow in get_section_list(). The static array tmp_section_list has space for 100 char* pointers (800 bytes), but the loop exit condition on line 1075 uses sizeof(tmp_section_list) (800) instead of sizeof(tmp_section_list)/sizeof(char*) (100). This allows the loop to write 4x past the array boundary when many colon-separated sections are provided via -S.

You are in the project's source directory. The buggy binary is at `./man`.
Key source file(s): src/man.c.

Crash reproduction: `./man -S ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::... ls`

The binary was compiled with AddressSanitizer (-fsanitize=address). Running it with the crashing input will produce an ASan report showing the exact overflow location.

Use bash to investigate: read the source, reproduce the crash, run gdb in batch mode, etc. Identify the root cause and propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.
queries.prompt:
You're debugging a bug in `man-overflow`, a real C program from the BugBench benchmark suite.

man-1.5h1 global buffer overflow in get_section_list(). The static array tmp_section_list has space for 100 char* pointers (800 bytes), but the loop exit condition on line 1075 uses sizeof(tmp_section_list) (800) instead of sizeof(tmp_section_list)/sizeof(char*) (100). This allows the loop to write 4x past the array boundary when many colon-separated sections are provided via -S.

You are in the project's source directory. The buggy binary is at `./man`.
Key source file(s): src/man.c.

Crash reproduction: `./man -S ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::... ls`

The binary was compiled with AddressSanitizer (-fsanitize=address). Running it with the crashing input will produce an ASan report showing the exact overflow location.

Use bash to investigate: read the source, reproduce the crash, run gdb in batch mode, etc. Identify the root cause and propose both a local fix and a structural global fix.

tool_calls.call: ./man -S $(for i in $(seq 1 400); do echo -n ':'; done) ls
./src/man -S $(for i in $(seq 1 400); do echo -n ':'; done) ls

cat <<'CHATDBG_DIAG_EOF'
ROOT CAUSE: In src/man.c:1075, the loop in get_section_list uses sizeof(tmp_section_list) instead of sizeof(tmp_section_list)/sizeof(char*), allowing the loop to continue beyond the array bounds when there are more than 100 sections provided via -S.

LOCAL FIX: Change the condition on line 1075 from "i+1 == sizeof(tmp_section_list)" to "i == sizeof(tmp_section_list)/sizeof(char*)". This prevents assigning beyond the 100-element array.

GLOBAL FIX: Replace the fixed-size static array tmp_section_list with a dynamically allocated array that can grow as needed, e.g., using realloc to handle an arbitrary number of sections, preventing buffer overflows from too many sections.
CHATDBG_DIAG_EOF
